definition: Household sector is structurally insulated (hedge financing) via ~95% mortgage debt locked below 5% and debt service ratio ~9.7% (vs. 13.2% peak Q3 2007). Corporate/financial leverage is advancing toward speculative/Ponzi: 40%+ of Russell 2000 unprofitable, covenant-lite >90% of leveraged loans, PIK toggle proliferation. Hedge share ~55-60%, down from ~70% in 2019.
evidence: PIK toggle proliferation in private credit
Hedge share ~55-60%, down from ~70% in 2019, but below 2007 peaks
Hedge financing share declining from ~70% to ~55-60%
Pair_2 debate endorsed novel crisis topology as genuinely unprecedented
open_questions: Is a financial crisis that doesn't propagate to households unprecedented, and what would that look like?
How does private credit opacity ($1.8T) affect the true Minsky classification of the corporate sector?
Could corporate Ponzi financing unwind while household sector remains in hedge mode?
Can a corporate/financial Minsky moment with insulated households propagate into a systemic crisis through channels not visible in 2001?
What is the threshold of corporate distress required to breach household insulation?
